sources.name: DLUHC
sources.dataset: Council Tax Base: Local Authority Level Data (Table CTB1)

metadata.methodology: Long-term empty homes are defined as properties that have been unoccupied and substantially unfurnished for over six months, as reported through council tax records. Total empty homes include both short- and long-term vacancies. Data is collected annually from local authority council tax base returns.
knownIssues: Council tax records may undercount empty homes where owners claim single-person discounts fraudulently
Definition of 'long-term empty' varies slightly between local authorities
Second homes are excluded from empty homes counts but overlap exists in coastal areas
COVID-19 disrupted council tax reporting in 2020/21
